blocks.content: His grandmother was on the phone when he came in through the screen door.
The house smelled like floor cleaner and coffee and the close warm smell of a house that had been shut against the rain. The kitchen light was on. Something on the stove, covered.
She was at the kitchen table with the phone cord stretched across the room — Beverly, from the look of it. Beverly required a long cord and a patient expression and about forty-five minutes.
He took them off at the door.
He pulled it over his head.
She pointed at the utility closet.
She held out her hand without looking at him. He gave her the wet shirt. She accepted it with two fingers, like evidence.
He made himself a plate from the stove and sat across from her. She was still on with Beverly. He ate.
When she hung up she looked at him with the expression she reserved for things she'd already figured out.
